title: RIO Receipt Protocol — Consolidated Specification
description: Defines the complete chain of custody for governed AI actions: ActionRequest → AIRecommendation → ApprovalRecord → ExecutionRecord → Receipt. Each schema is self-contained. The Receipt binds all preceding artifacts into a single cryptographically signed, hash-chained proof.

ActionRequest.description: An immutable record of a proposed action submitted by an AI agent or upstream system. Once created, the request cannot be modified.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this action request.

action_type.description: The type of action being requested.

requested_by.description: Identifier of the AI agent or system that submitted this request.

target.description: The target system or resource the action will affect.

parameters.description: Action-specific parameters. Structure varies by action_type.

risk_level.description: Assessed risk level of the proposed action.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this request was created.

context.description: Optional free-text context explaining why this action is being requested.

AIRecommendation.description: The AI system's evaluation of an ActionRequest against the active policy context. States whether the action should proceed, be blocked, or be escalated.

request_id.description: The request_id of the ActionRequest being evaluated.

recommended_action.description: The AI's recommendation for how to handle this request.

confidence.description: Confidence percentage (0-100) in the recommendation. Actions below the configured threshold are automatically escalated to human review.

reasoning.description: Explanation of the factors considered in making this recommendation.

unknowns.description: Explicit declaration of assumptions or unknowns that affect confidence.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this recommendation was generated.

ApprovalRecord.description: A cryptographically signed, time-limited authorization from a human authority. Each approval is bound to a specific request_id and can only be used once.

approval_id.description: Unique identifier for this approval record.

request_id.description: The request_id of the ActionRequest being approved or denied.

approver_id.description: Identifier of the human who issued this approval.

approver_role.description: The organizational role of the approver at the time of approval.

approval_status.description: The outcome of the human authorization decision.

approved_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the human issued this approval.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp after which this approval is no longer valid. Default TTL: 300 seconds.

signature.description: Cryptographic signature over the canonical form of this approval record.

auth_method.description: The authentication method used to verify the approver's identity.

notes.description: Optional notes from the approver explaining the decision.

ExecutionRecord.description: Records the outcome of an action executed after receiving a valid, unexpired, unused ApprovalRecord.

execution_id.description: Unique identifier for this execution record.

request_id.description: The request_id of the ActionRequest that was executed.

approval_id.description: The approval_id that authorized this execution. Must be valid, unexpired, and unused.

executed_by.description: Identifier of the agent or system that performed the execution.

executed_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when execution completed.

execution_status.description: The outcome of the execution attempt.

target_result.description: The result returned by the target system. Structure is action_type-specific.

execution_token_id.description: A one-time token consumed during execution. Proves authorization and prevents replay.

error_detail.description: If execution_status is failed or partial, a description of what went wrong.

Receipt.description: The final, immutable record of a completed governed action. Binds together the request, recommendation, approval, and execution into a single signed, hash-chained artifact. A valid receipt proves the full chain was followed and the outcome is non-repudiable.

request_id.description: The request_id of the ActionRequest this receipt covers.

recommendation_id.description: The recommendation_id of the AIRecommendation produced for this request.

approval_id.description: The approval_id of the ApprovalRecord that authorized execution.

execution_id.description: The execution_id of the ExecutionRecord that records what was done.

action_type.description: The action_type from the original ActionRequest.

requested_by.description: The requesting identity from the original ActionRequest.

approver_id.description: The approver identity from the ApprovalRecord.

executed_by.description: The executor identity from the ExecutionRecord.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this receipt was generated.

ledger_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the canonical JSON of this receipt (excluding ledger_hash and signature). Used to verify receipt integrity.

previous_hash.description: The ledger_hash of the preceding receipt. For the first receipt, this value is 'GENESIS'. Creates the tamper-evident hash chain.

chain_index.description: Zero-based position of this receipt in the ledger chain.

signature.description: Cryptographic signature over the canonical form of this receipt (including ledger_hash, excluding signature).

verification_method.description: The cryptographic method used to produce and verify the signature.

invariants: No execution without approved request: The execution gateway must fail-closed.
No valid receipt without signed approval and execution record.
All high-risk actions must be provable: Any auditor can verify the full chain.
Single-use approvals: Each approval token is consumed exactly once.

hash_specification.canonicalization: Deterministic JSON serialization: keys sorted alphabetically, no whitespace, UTF-8 encoding
